notes: Pipeline gap fixed: data/universe.json had never been built since the 06-15 strategy pivot, leaving 4 straight sessions with an empty watchlist. Ran scripts/build_universe.py (516 tickers: 503 S&P 500 + 101 NASDAQ-100, deduplicated) -- required two fixes: Wikipedia now blocks requests without a User-Agent header (added one), and the NASDAQ-100 table parse choked on a non-string-column table (made the ticker-column lookup defensive). evening_scan.py also had a latent bug: one invalid symbol (BF-B, and BRK-B) failing inside a 50-symbol batch was killing all 50 quotes for that batch every run since chunks are deterministic and alphabetical -- added a drop-invalid-and-retry loop, recovering coverage from 466/516 to 514/516 tickers and surfacing a third candidate (CDNS) that the bug had been silently hiding. Of the 3 survivors: MGM is the strongest setup (RS 1.30 well above the 1.10 floor, vol dry-up to 0.48 is excellent, but VCP 0.72 and 7.0% off the 52w high are both near the edges of their thresholds rather than deep in ideal territory). None of the three 52w-high distances (6.5-7.0%) look like a spike day; each closed mildly below a clean prior-day high. Market context: SPY closed -0.54% today (VIX 16.1, calm), so these setups are coiling against a soft tape -- entry agent should weight the breakout-confirmation gate carefully tomorrow given the recent SPY pullback.
